assertions.title: Zero test failures across all Python versions

steps.action: Run pytest with --ignore flags on Python 3.12
steps.expected: Zero failures (only passes, skips, xpasses allowed)
steps.observed: 984 passed, 16 skipped, 1 xpassed, 10 warnings in 23.95s — exit code 0

steps.action: Run pytest with --ignore flags on Python 3.13
steps.expected: Zero failures (only passes, skips, xpasses allowed)
steps.observed: 984 passed, 16 skipped, 1 xpassed, 10 warnings in 34.34s — exit code 0

steps.action: Run pytest with --ignore flags on Python 3.14
steps.expected: Zero failures (only passes, skips, xpasses allowed)
steps.observed: 984 passed, 16 skipped, 1 xpassed, 10 warnings in 28.11s — exit code 0

evidence.python312: 984 passed, 16 skipped, 1 xpassed, 10 warnings — exit code 0
evidence.python313: 984 passed, 16 skipped, 1 xpassed, 10 warnings — exit code 0
evidence.python314: 984 passed, 16 skipped, 1 xpassed, 10 warnings — exit code 0
evidence.consoleErrors: none (only pytest warnings about destrieux atlas missing labels — pre-existing, non-fatal)

frictions.description: The .venv directory was in a broken state (no bin/ directory) from a previous run, causing 'no module named pytest' errors. Had to remove and recreate it with `uv sync --python 3.12 --extra dev`.
frictions.resolution: Removed .venv and ran uv sync with --extra dev to install dev dependencies (pytest). Subsequent runs for 3.13 and 3.14 used `uv run --extra dev` directly.

summary: Tested 1 assertion: VAL-FIX-005 passes. Full test suite produced zero failures on Python 3.12, 3.13, and 3.14. Each version yielded 984 passed, 16 skipped, 1 xpassed with exit code 0.
